user_message: I need you to process this email thread from Tailspin Toys. Multiple stakeholders are involved -- it's complex.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-09 12:17 EST
Subject: whitespace analysis - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about whitespace analysis. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-01-26 14:29 EST
Subject: URGENT: whitespace analysis Issue at Tailspin Toys

There's been a change in our timeline for whitespace analysis. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Tailspin Toys: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-M trap (No native 'cross-sell signal' primitive distinct from generic signal-to-opportun); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-M failure mode (No native 'cross-sell signal' primitive distinct from generic signal-to-opportun)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-M -- No native 'cross-sell signal' primitive distinct from generic signal-to-opportun
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
